## reference · Design Systems

* A design system is a complete set of standards to manage design at scale, reducing redundancy and creating a shared language and visual consistency across different pages and channels.
* Design systems provide benefits including:
  * Quick and scalable design and development work
  * Reduced strain on design resources, allowing focus on complex problems
  * Unified language within and between cross-functional teams
  * Visual consistency across products, channels, and departments
  * Educational tool and reference for junior designers and content contributors
* Potential hurdles to using a design system:
  * Time-intensive creation and maintenance
  * Need for dedicated team to manage and teach the design system
  * Perception that projects are static and don't require reusable components
* A design system consists of:
  * A design repository containing:
    * Style guide with implementation guidelines, visual references, and design principles
    * Component library with reusable UI elements, including:
      + Component name
      + Description
      + Attributes
      + State
      + Code snippets
      + Front-end and back-end frameworks
    * Pattern library with collections of UI-element groupings or layouts
  * A team to manage the design system, including:
    * Interaction designer
    * Visual designer
    * Developer
    * Optional: researcher, architect, content writer, and executive sponsor
* Approaches to design-system adoption:
  * Adopting an existing design system (lowest-cost, least time-consuming)
  * Adapting an existing design system
  * Creating a custom design system (most time-consuming and costly)

Sources: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/design-systems-101/
